name: Peak XV Partners
description: India's largest and most active VC fund, formerly Sequoia India. Backs companies from early to late stage across all sectors. Known for long-term conviction bets.
thesis: Back exceptional founders addressing large markets with a product or service that is 10x better than alternatives.

name: Accel India
description: One of the most active early-stage investors in India. Has backed Flipkart, Freshworks, and BrowserStack. Known for strong founder relationships and global network.
thesis: Partner with founders at the earliest stages, especially in B2B and enterprise software, and support their global expansion ambitions.

name: Lightspeed India
description: Part of global Lightspeed network. Active in consumer and enterprise in India. Known for backing OYO, Udaan, and Darwinbox.
thesis: Invest in founders with an insight-driven approach to transforming large markets in India, across consumer and enterprise.

name: Nexus Venture Partners
description: India-US VC focused on building global product companies from India. Strong thesis on SaaS and cloud infrastructure.
thesis: Partner with Indian founders building global software products, leveraging India's engineering talent to serve world markets.

name: Blume Ventures
description: India's leading early-stage VC with a focus on the first institutional cheque. Known for backing Unacademy, Slice, and Dunzo early.
thesis: Be the first institutional partner for ambitious Indian founders, providing capital, conviction, and community.

name: Kalaari Capital
description: Women-led fund (Vani Kola) with deep roots in the Indian consumer internet ecosystem. Backed Dream11 and Cure.fit.
thesis: Back the next generation of Indian consumer companies that deeply understand and serve the aspirations of young India.

name: 3one4 Capital
description: Research-driven early stage VC known for contrarian bets. Backed Locus, Open (neobank), and Setu. Strong LP network in Silicon Valley.
thesis: Back founders who approach problems with first-principles thinking and have an unfair advantage—technology, insight, or distribution.

name: Z47 (Matrix Partners India)
description: Rebranded from Matrix Partners India in 2024. Known for backing Ola and Razorpay. Strong operator network across India.
thesis: Partner with category-defining companies at Series A/B, when there is early proof of product-market fit.

name: Elevation Capital
description: One of the oldest India-focused VCs with a portfolio spanning Swiggy, ShareChat, and Paytm. Independent fund since 2022 after separation from GGV.
thesis: Invest across stages in companies that will define the next decade of Indian digital consumption and enterprise technology.

name: Chiratae Ventures
description: Formerly IDG Ventures India. One of India's oldest VCs with 17+ years of experience. Known for Firstcry and Lenskart IPOs.
thesis: Invest in category-creating Indian companies that address fundamental gaps in the Indian consumer and enterprise landscape.

name: Stellaris Venture Partners
description: Founded by ex-Helion Venture Partners GPs. Strong focus on B2B and SaaS companies targeting global markets from India.
thesis: Back founders at the intersection of insight + distribution advantage, especially in B2B SaaS going global.

name: Fireside Ventures
description: India's premier consumer brand-focused VC. Backed Mamaearth from early stage. Specializes in building D2C consumer brands in India.
thesis: Back the next generation of purpose-led consumer brands that solve genuine consumer pain, with a digital-first approach.

name: Omnivore Partners
description: India's only specialist agri-food-climate VC. Deep expertise in farm-to-fork innovation and rural India. Backed by DFIs and impact investors.
thesis: Build a profitable, sustainable food and agriculture system in India that benefits 600M+ farmers and rural communities.

name: India Quotient
description: Specializes in Bharat-focused startups — products for the next 500M Indian internet users in Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities. Backed ShareChat and Meesho.
thesis: The next wave of Indian internet will be driven by regional language, vernacular media, and social commerce. We back those founders.

name: 100X.VC
description: India's leading first-cheque VC. Invests iSAFE notes in 100 startups per year. Strong Mumbai-based network. Known for high-volume, early-stage bets.
thesis: Every startup deserves a first institutional investor. We write the first cheque, fast, with minimal dilution, using iSAFE.

name: pi Ventures
description: India's leading deep tech and AI-focused VC. Specializes in companies where AI and ML are the core product differentiation, not just a feature.
thesis: Back technical founders building products where AI/ML is the moat, not just a feature — from robotics to healthcare AI.

name: Antler India
description: Global VC that helps individuals build companies. Runs cohort programs in Bengaluru where talented individuals are matched as co-founders and funded from day 1.
thesis: The hardest part of starting a startup is finding the right co-founder. We solve that and fund the team from day zero.

name: Prime Venture Partners
description: Founded by serial entrepreneurs. Known for hands-on mentorship and operator experience. Backed Niyo, Happay (acquired by CRED), and MyGate.
thesis: Bring operator experience and network to founders solving fundamental India-specific problems in fintech and consumer software.

name: Fundamentum Partnership
description: Founded by Nandan Nilekani (Infosys co-founder, Aadhaar architect) and Sanjeev Aggarwal. Focuses on Indian mid-market B2B and tech-enabled services.
thesis: Back Indian companies serving the mid-market opportunity at Series A/B with proven product-market fit and a clear path to profitability.

name: Venture Catalysts (VCats)
description: India's largest and most active angel investor platform. 400+ HNI investors across 6 cities. Also runs 9Unicorns accelerator.
thesis: Democratize early-stage investing in India by pooling HNI capital into diverse startup bets across all sectors.

name: Axilor Ventures
description: Founded by Kris Gopalakrishnan (Infosys) and SD Shibulal. Runs structured accelerator programs + seed fund. Strong Infosys alumni mentor network.
thesis: Help first-time founders cross the valley of death from idea to Series A through structured acceleration and early capital.

name: Bharat Innovation Fund
description: Hyderabad-based deep tech VC with strong connections to IIT Hyderabad, IIIT Hyderabad, and CSIR labs. Focus on R&D-heavy startups solving India-specific problems.
thesis: Back Indian deeptech that solves fundamental problems in healthcare, agriculture, energy, and defence with proprietary IP.

name: Iron Pillar
description: Growth-stage specialist with deep expertise in B2B and enterprise software. Backed Zenoti (unicorn), Cashfree, and BetterPlace.
thesis: Scale companies that have proven PMF and unit economics, helping them expand from India to global markets.

name: Ankur Capital
description: Impact-focused VC investing in technology companies addressing underserved markets in India — especially rural, agricultural, and low-income segments.
thesis: Build profitable businesses that create measurable positive impact for underserved Indian communities through technology.

name: Omidyar Network India
description: Part of Pierre Omidyar (eBay founder)'s philanthropy network. Combines grants and equity investments in companies and nonprofits creating social impact in India.
thesis: Invest in companies and nonprofits creating pathways to greater individual opportunity and societal impact across financial access, digital public infrastructure, and livelihoods.

name: Quona Capital
description: Global FinTech impact investor focused on financial inclusion for underserved populations across India, Southeast Asia, and Africa.
thesis: FinTech for the underserved: invest in companies that bring affordable, accessible financial services to low-income and underbanked populations.

name: Titan Capital
description: Fund by Sarthak Ahuja and Utsav Somani. Known for early checks in 200+ companies including CoinDCX and Urban Company. Strong community of founders.
thesis: Back bold founders at pre-seed and seed who have a contrarian insight and can move fast to capture emerging Indian consumer trends.

name: WaterBridge Ventures
description: Founded by ex-LendingKart co-founder. Focused on early stage, pre-PMF companies. Known for high conviction bets on contrarian founders.
thesis: Write the first institutional cheque in founders who are 2-3 years ahead of the market in understanding a problem.

name: General Catalyst (India Portfolio)
description: One of the world's top VCs (Airbnb, Stripe, Canva). Expanding India presence. Led by Hemant Taneja globally. Invested in BlueSmart, DeHaat, and more.
thesis: Invest in companies that use technology for responsible innovation — solving climate, health, and access challenges at scale.

name: B Capital Group (India)
description: Global growth-stage VC co-founded by Eduardo Saverin (Facebook co-founder). Strong BCG consulting network. Backed Darwinbox and Qure.ai in India.
thesis: Growth-stage investing in enterprise and healthcare technology companies with global ambitions, leveraging BCG's operational network.

name: Inflection Point Ventures (IPV)
description: India's largest angel network with 7,000+ investors. Runs monthly deal reviews and backs 10–12 startups per month. Open application process.
thesis: Connect high-quality startups with a curated network of 7,000+ HNI and angel investors across India.

name: Sprout Venture Partners
description: Seed-stage VC with focus on consumer and D2C startups. Run by ex-founders with deep operational experience in building brands.
thesis: Back first-time founders with deep category insights and unfair distribution advantages in consumer tech and D2C.

name: Sixth Sense Ventures
description: India consumer-focused growth VC with strong retail and FMCG industry network. LP base includes leading Indian business families.
thesis: Back proven consumer businesses at Series A/B that are ready to scale distribution across India's 700M middle class.

name: Unicorn India Ventures
description: Early-stage VC focused on deep tech and B2B. Runs sector-specific cohorts and annual Unicorn Battle competition for seed-stage startups.
thesis: Back founders who are building with technical differentiation in B2B and deep tech — sectors underserved by mainstream VCs.

name: Better Capital
description: Pre-seed VC and angel fund run by Vaibhav Domkundwar. Known for transparent founder-first approach and publishing investment thesis publicly. First-cheque focus.
thesis: Be the most founder-friendly first investor. Decide in days, not months. Invest in people first, ideas second.

name: Gruhas
description: Founded by Nikhil Kamath (Zerodha) and Abhijeet Pai. Focuses on real estate tech, proptech, construction tech, and sustainability. Young fund with strong founder network.
thesis: The built environment is the next frontier for tech disruption. Back founders building the future of how India lives, works, and builds.

name: Incubate Fund India
description: Japanese-origin early stage VC with India operations. Connects Indian startups to Japan and Asia market opportunities. Strong network of Japanese corporates as enterprise customers.
thesis: Help Indian B2B and deep tech startups access the Japanese and APAC enterprise market through our corporate partnerships.

name: Matrix Partners India
description: Affiliated with Matrix Partners USA. Deep partner engagement model. Backed Ola, Dailyhunt, Mswipe.
thesis: Partner with founders early and stay long-term. Focus on large markets and disruptive business models.

name: Stellaris Venture Partners
description: Seed-focused VC with deep operator experience. Known for early bets in B2B SaaS.
thesis: Invest early in product-led, capital-efficient businesses with global potential.

name: Global Founders Capital (GFC)
description: Founded by Rocket Internet alumni (Oliver and Marc Samwer). Active in India. Fast decisions.
thesis: Invest early in internet companies building large consumer or SMB platforms.

name: Fundamentum Partnership
description: Nandan Nilekani and Sanjeev Aggarwal-backed growth fund. Focuses on Series A-C.
thesis: Provide scale-up capital to category leaders with proven product-market fit.

name: Sequoia Arc (Early Stage Program)
description: Peak XV (Sequoia India) accelerator program. 16-week cohort with $1-2M at pre-seed. Highly competitive.
thesis: Find founders before they find product-market fit and co-build the company with them.

name: ah! Ventures
description: Democratizing angel investing in India. Platform + fund model with 4000+ angel investors.
thesis: Democratize access to early-stage capital for Indian startups beyond tier-1 cities.

name: Venture Highway
description: Early-stage VC backed by Neeraj Arora (ex-WhatsApp BD). Strong consumer internet thesis.
thesis: Back mission-driven founders at seed stage. Focus on billion-dollar market opportunities.

name: Surge (Sequoia / Peak XV)
description: Peak XV's rapid scale-up program for early-stage founders. 16-week cohort, $1-2M SAFE.
thesis: Build with founders from idea to product-market fit, not just write a cheque.

name: BEENEXT
description: Japan-backed early-stage VC. Strong Southeast Asia and India presence. Backed BharatPe, Slice.
thesis: Partner with the most ambitious founders in emerging markets from day one.

name: Sherpalo Ventures
description: Ram Shriram's fund (original Google angel investor). Deep US-India corridor network.
thesis: Invest in breakthrough ideas at the intersection of US/India with a focus on long-term value.
